stories.title: Suspect arrested after allegedly yelling ‘Allahu Akbar,’ stabbing 2 men in NYC attack
stories.summary: A 51-year-old suspect, Raul Morales, was arrested after allegedly shouting "Allahu Akbar" while stabbing two men in Manhattan’s Upper West Side on Thursday afternoon. Both victims, aged 57 and 50, were taken to the hospital in stable condition after separate attacks near Central Park West and Amsterdam Avenue. Authorities are evaluating the incidents as potential hate crimes while investigating Morales’ motive.

stories.title: Avatar: Seven Havens Officially Reveals Korra's Earthbender Successor With October 9 Premiere
stories.summary: Avatar: Seven Havens premieres on October 9, 2026, introducing Pavi, the new Earthbending Avatar successor to Korra, in a fractured world that blames the Avatar for a past catastrophe. The 26-episode series, co-created by Michael Dante DiMartino and Bryan Konietzko, follows Pavi and her twin sister Nisha as they uncover their origins and defend the last strongholds of civilization.

stories.title: Samantha Morton casts a spell and steals 'The Odyssey.' She's always been a little dangerous
stories.summary: Samantha Morton's captivating performance as Circe in Christopher Nolan's adaptation of "The Odyssey" has left audiences spellbound, particularly in a haunting scene where she transforms Odysseus' men into pigs using her hands, a visceral and unsettling sequence that relies on practical effects. Morton, known for her intense shape-shifting performances, brings a depth to the character that makes her transformation of the men all the more believable. The film, which opened in theaters this weekend, marks a significant milestone in Morton's illustrious career.

stories.title: UPDATE: Verdict reached in jury trial of Shiloh Hendrix
stories.summary: A jury has found Shiloh Hendrix not guilty of disorderly conduct involving a child but guilty of disorderly conduct against a camera operator during an April 2025 incident in Rochester, Minnesota. Hendrix received supervised probation, 200 hours of community service, and a $1,000 fine. The trial centered on allegations that she used a racial slur toward a Black child.

stories.title: The Game Takes a Bottle to the Head at 'VERZUZ' With YG
stories.summary: The Game stunned viewers at Thursday’s VERZUZ showdown with YG by downing nearly an entire bottle of tequila in one gulp. The Compton rappers traded hits during the two-round battle in front of a live crowd. The event, created by Swizz Beatz and Timbaland in 2020, continues to showcase hip-hop legends in high-energy face-offs.

stories.title: Behind the Bylines: The stories beyond the game
stories.summary: The World Cup final on Sunday delivered a dramatic finish as Argentina secured victory over Spain in a penalty shootout. The match capped months of global anticipation, with fans worldwide glued to their screens. Analysts now dissect how tactical shifts and individual brilliance shaped the outcome.

stories.title: 'Evidence lacking' for mental health monitoring tech
stories.summary: A UK inquiry has found no strong evidence that Oxevision, a monitoring system used in mental health wards, improves patient safety. The Lampard Inquiry, examining over 2,000 deaths in Essex mental health services since 2000, heard experts criticise the lack of robust independent research. The technology’s maker claims it helped staff respond to 1,453 safety incidents in one trust over four years.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners, citing forced labour concerns
stories.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer has announced the imposition of new tariffs on 60 trading partners, accounting for the majority of US imports, due to concerns over forced labour practices. The tariffs, ranging from 10% to 12.5%, target key economic partners including the UK, China, the European Union, and India, and came into effect on Friday. This move marks the latest escalation in the global trade war, as the US seeks to address human rights abuses and distortive trade practices.

stories.title: Please Welcome Demi Moore to the Humungous-Hat Club
stories.summary: Demi Moore has joined the exclusive Humongous-Hat Club, a group of celebrities who make a bold fashion statement with oversized headpieces. The actress wore a massive, cloud-like organza hat to a private shopping appointment at the Schiaparelli showroom in Paris during haute couture week, where she was accompanied by her daughter Tallulah Willis. This extravagant accessory is the latest addition to Moore's impressive wardrobe, solidifying her status as a fashion icon.

stories.title: A Million-Panel Project
stories.summary: Utah’s Green River Energy Center, a massive solar and battery storage plant, went online in June 2026 with nearly one million solar panels and 500 batteries. The facility delivers 400 megawatts of solar power and 400 megawatts of storage, supplying energy to Salt Lake City and beyond. It marks a shift from the region’s coal mining past to renewable energy.

stories.title: Burnham urged to 'build bridges' with sacked Starmerites
stories.summary: Andy Burnham needs to "build bridges" with the Starmerite minister he sacked during his reshuffle, Baroness Harriet Harman has told the Electoral Dysfunction podcast.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
stories.summary: Sia’s 2016 concert film Nostalgic for the Present showcases her iconic anthems like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" through a faceless, abstract performance that amplifies themes of feminine strength. The film highlights how her anonymity transforms her into a vessel for universal empowerment.

stories.title: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
stories.summary: Yemen’s Houthi rebels have attacked Red Sea shipping for 13 straight nights, forcing vessels to reroute and threatening the main Europe-Asia trade lane. US Central Command struck 13 Iranian targets overnight, citing threats to Gulf maritime traffic. Analysts warn the escalation risks deeper regional conflict and higher global shipping costs.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will spend his first day in office working from the new No 10 North in Manchester, where he will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council, a cabinet committee launched in 2008 to address UK economic challenges. The meeting will bring together senior ministers and regional mayors to focus on devolution and local economic growth, marking a shift of power from Whitehall and Westminster to regional areas. Up to 300 civil servants are expected to join the new site by the end of next year.

stories.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
stories.summary: NASA’s Curiosity rover completed its 4,960th Martian day on July 27, 2026, analyzing five new rock targets with its APXS instrument and MAHLI imager. Engineers overcame dusty, uneven terrain by safely deploying the robotic arm after brushing surfaces. The team continues driving toward sulfate-rich layers to study Mars’ ancient climate shifts.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: Indonesian babies trafficked to Singapore for illegal adoption face uncertain futures after a court convicted 19 people for procuring at least 34 infants. Twelve of the babies were sent to Singapore, leaving adoptive parents in limbo as authorities decide their fate. Both governments are now under pressure to resolve the children’s status swiftly.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals, in the first major action under the EU's Digital Markets Act. The Commission argued this limited consumer choice and gave Google's services an unfair advantage, with the fine comprised of two separate breaches of the act. Google will now have to strip away features and dismantle safety protections to comply with the EU's requirements.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: The Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow featured a surprise appearance by King Charles III, who arrived via a Tardis-like lift. Scotland’s team received the loudest cheer as they marched in kilts, followed by a Loch Ness Monster float and a rousing performance of Flower of Scotland. The show celebrated Glasgow’s culture with live music and a dance under a replica of the city’s iconic clock.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR inflicted more derby misery on Hull FC, winning 28-20 in a Super League match that saw the away side come from behind to secure a crucial victory. The win marked Hull KR's ninth consecutive derby win and moved them back into the play-off positions in fifth place. The result was a sixth straight loss for second-bottom Hull FC, whose struggling season continues.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.
